name: Queen’s Court of Psanan
summary: High ceremonial and political court anchoring Psanan’s matriarchal authority and formal judgments.
day_to_day: Hears major disputes, ratifies public oaths, and sets civic tone during crisis or rebuilding.
who_works_there: Matriarchal judges, heralds, oath keepers, security retinues, scribes.
who_uses_it: Great Houses, claimants, oath bearers, civic petitioners.
authority: High authority over formal dispute, sanction, and public legitimacy in Psanan.
public_manifestation: Court processions, ash-chamber hearings, posted decrees, witnessed vows.
conflict_or_bottleneck: Ceremonial power can become brittle when practical scarcity outruns rhetoric.
